pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 122 shows TCP Listener on Port 6661; task description confirms this is the NextGen Connect channel listening port for receiving HL7 ORM messages
setup_task.sh lines 16-17 define Lab Simulator PORT=6670; line 57 shows server listening on this port; task sends messages to localhost:6670
setup_task.sh line 86 connects to Docker container 'nextgen-postgres' with database 'mirthdb'; JDBC URL format matches standard PostgreSQL connection string with default port 5432

setup_task.sh line 103 defines HL7 message with Control ID at MSH-10 field (array index 9 after split): 'MSH|^~\&|HIS|HOSPITAL|LAB|QUEST|202403151000||ORM^O01|ORD-001|P|2.3' - verified via Python split yielding fields[9]='ORD-001'; also appears in ORC and OBR segments; database seeded with this ID in lab_orders table

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic healthcare integration task. The NextGen Connect integration engine receives HL7 ORM lab order messages on TCP port 6661, forwards them to a lab simulator listening on port 6670 which responds with ACK messages containing reference IDs, and updates a PostgreSQL database at jdbc:postgresql://nextgen-postgres:5432/mirthdb. The sample order file contains HL7 v2.3 message with Control ID ORD-001, generated synthetically by setup scripts. All infrastructure, messages, and test data are created programmatically during task initialization.
